events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: sf-interview-me-explicit

You are completing a user request. The following Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE to you and you should apply it if it fits the request. Treat it as an active skill you have invoked.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when the user has a half-baked software or product idea and wants to flesh it out, validate it, pressure-test it, sharpen it, or "interview me about my idea" before designing or building.

Core principle: An idea gets stronger from ONE question at a time, adaptive depth, and an ADVERSARIAL stress-test — NOT a batch of questions and a polished plan you wrote yourself. Cover every dimension, drill only the weak ones, attack the idea before documenting it, then HAND OFF — do not write the execution plan inline.

Iron Rules: (1) One question per message, never batch. (2) You do NOT write the execution plan; output is a strengthened idea brief then a handoff. (3) The adversarial phase is mandatory. (4) Cover all 8 dimensions.

8 Dimensions: Problem; Target user; Value proposition; Differentiation; Scope/MVP; Feasibility; Risks & assumptions; Validation.

4-Phase Engine: Phase 1 breadth scan — rate all 8 dims solid/shaky/missing from the pitch, ask at most 1–2 broad questions, END by showing the user a scorecard table. Phase 2 adaptive deep-dives — Socratic, one question at a time, only on shaky/missing dims; may offer a sharpening suggestion; reflect each answer back. Phase 3 adversarial stress-test (mandatory) — strongest objection, riskiest hidden assumption, "why hasn't someone done this / why now", scope-creep check, one at a time. Phase 4 synthesis — write the brief, confirm, save to ./idea-briefs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md, then hand off to superpowers:brainstorming. Do not write scope/timeline/architecture yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
I have an idea for an app but it's pretty half-baked. Can you interview me about my idea before I start building anything?

Produce the actual response you would send to the user (your first turn — this is an interactive multi-turn skill, so respond as you would to open the interaction). Then return the deterministic result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" containing the full text of your response.

The task is: open the bake-to-completion interview skill by responding to a user who wants to be interviewed about their half-baked app idea. This is Phase 1 — I need to invite them to share the pitch before I can score the 8 dimensions. I cannot ask 8 questions at once; I ask one question (or a very short opener) to get them talking.

Adversarial check on my planned response:
- Am I batching questions? No — I'm asking them to share the idea first, which is the only sensible opener before any dimensions can be assessed.
- Am I jumping to advice or a plan? No.
- Am I faithfully representing the skill without gold-plating? Yes.
- Is there anything under-specified? The skill says Phase 1 starts with rating the 8 dims from the pitch — I can't rate until I have a pitch. So the correct first move is a single open invitation.

```json
{
  "caseId": "sf-interview-me-explicit",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "I'd love to help you pressure-test and sharpen your idea before you write a single line of code.\n\nTo kick things off: what's the idea? Give me whatever you have — even a rough sentence or two is enough to get started. Don't worry about it being polished.",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": true,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Recognized this as Phase 1 of the bake-to-completion skill",
      "Identified that no pitch has been shared yet, so no 8-dimension scorecard can be produced",
      "Issued a single open invitation for the user to share their pitch — the only valid first move before any dimension can be rated",
      "Did not batch questions, did not write a plan, did not jump ahead to deep-dives"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user has not yet stated their idea — they only said they have one; the pitch must be elicited before Phase 1 scoring can begin",
      "A brief, warm single-question opener is the correct first turn per the skill's 'one question per message' iron rule"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Cannot produce the 8-dimension scorecard until the user shares their idea — this response is the necessary precursor to Phase 1, not Phase 1 itself"
    ]
  }
}
```
